candidate_llm_application: Floriography compositional-adversarial-prompt detector (defence-side; learned dictionary + positional decoder + multi-source bouquet scan over prompt + tool-call history + file metadata)

results.justification: Source-domain encyclopedia on floriography/selam. Provides only the source metaphor, not the LLM-side detector. No LLM, no adversarial, no compositional prompt-scan content.
Source-domain article on Victorian tussie-mussie practice. No LLM content. Source-side keyword only.
Source-domain article on floriography dictionary. Source metaphor only; no LLM, no detector.

Steganographic LLM jailbreaks: ATTACK-side mechanism for hiding harmful intent inside benign cover text via compositional encoding. Same mechanism as floriography but on attack side; candidate proposes defence-side detector. Func equivalence is on opposite side; sem is moderate (shared mechanism, opposite role).

Hide and Seek in Embedding Space: explicitly Steganography AND Detection in LLM embedding geometry. This is a defence-side detector of compositional/steganographic adversarial prompts; operationally near-equivalent to the proposed bouquet decoder, modulo specific implementation (geometry-based vs learned-dictionary-and-positional). Hits both sem>=0.7 and func>=0.7.
Semantic Intent Fragmentation Compositional Attack on MAS: attack-side. Candidate's novelty claim explicitly distinguishes attack from defence. Defence-side equivalent not provided here.

SecurityLingua: prompt-compression-based malicious-intent detector. Same broad defence goal but different mechanism (compression heuristic, not dictionary+positional bouquet decoder). Below func and sem thresholds.
Compositional Steering with Steering Tokens: behavior-control side via composition, not detection. Different goal (control vs detect adversarial). Below all thresholds.

notes: Independent re-judging confirms rank 5 (Hide and Seek in Embedding Space: Geometry-based Steganography & Detection, 2601.22818) is a defence-side compositional/steganographic adversarial-prompt detector that operationally overlaps the proposed floriography bouquet decoder (sem~0.72, func~0.74). Ranks 4 and 6 are attack-side mirror mechanisms; candidate explicitly carved out the defence-side as the novelty claim, so they do NOT count as functional hits at the 0.7 threshold here (their func~0.45-0.55). Rank 7 (SecurityLingua) is defence-side but uses prompt compression, a different mechanism, and falls under threshold. Total functional/semantic hits >=1 -> FAIL per FROZEN OR rule.
